mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment channels, driving robust corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, compressing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not precede negative 20-day returns, or the predictive coefficient flips positive in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, higher discount rates compress equity valuation multiples, triggering immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in cross-validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that increases corporate earnings expectations, directly translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across rolling 3-year windows, or the predictive t-statistic falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 115, it signals a severe contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruptions, prompting immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO drops below 115, or the threshold signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
